title: Safe Drinking Water Act
text: Federal statute (1974, amended 1986/1996). Establishes EPA authority to set NPDWR (National Primary Drinking Water Regulations) for contaminants posing health risk. Applies to all Public Water Systems (PWS) serving 25+ people or 15+ connections.

title: NPDWR contaminant classes
text: Two regulatory types: MCL (Maximum Contaminant Level — concentration-based) for measurable contaminants (lead, nitrate, arsenic, TTHM, HAA5, radium); TT (Treatment Technique) for contaminants where direct measurement isn't feasible (Surface Water Treatment Rule, Lead and Copper). Public Notification Tiers 1/2/3 by severity.

title: Lead and Copper Rule
text: Original 1991 LCR. Action Levels: lead 15 µg/L (90th percentile); copper 1.3 mg/L. Sampling at customer taps. AL exceedance triggers: optimized corrosion control, public education, lead service line replacement (LSLR).

title: LCRR effective Jan 2024 (inventory) + Oct 2024 (full)
text: LCRR adds: lead-service-line INVENTORY publication (Oct 16 2024 deadline), AL trigger of 10 µg/L (in addition to 15 µg/L AL), find-and-fix protocol for individual sites > 15 µg/L, school + childcare facility testing, public education for inventory results. LCRI further revisions proposed 2024.

title: Tier 1 Public Notification
text: Acute violations requiring 24-HOUR notification: E. coli MCL, nitrate MCL > 10 mg/L, nitrite > 1 mg/L, total nitrate+nitrite > 10, TT violation that increases risk of imminent illness, chlorine dioxide MRDL > 0.8, fluoride > 4 mg/L, exceedance of MCL where state authorities determine acute. Methods: media + door-to-door + electronic.

title: Tier 2 Public Notification
text: Non-acute MCL/TT violations requiring 30-day notification: lead AL exceedance, TTHM/HAA5/arsenic MCL, copper AL, Surface Water Treatment Rule violations, monitoring violations, variances. Methods: mail / direct delivery.

title: Tier 3 Public Notification
text: Annual Consumer Confidence Report (CCR) by July 1 each year for all community water systems. Covers prior calendar year monitoring + violations + source-water + special-population notes.

title: Disinfection Byproducts Rule (Stage 2)
text: MCL for Total Trihalomethanes (TTHM): 80 µg/L. MCL for sum of five Haloacetic Acids (HAA5): 60 µg/L. Locational Running Annual Average (LRAA) compliance basis. Initial Distribution System Evaluation (IDSE) to identify highest-risk sample points.

title: Surface Water Treatment Rule
text: TT requirement: 99.9% (3-log) Giardia removal/inactivation; 99% (2-log) Cryptosporidium; 99.99% (4-log) viruses. Filtration: turbidity ≤ 0.3 NTU in 95% of monthly samples for combined filter effluent. Disinfectant residual minimum at entry point + maintain in distribution.

title: America's Water Infrastructure Act §1433
text: Community water systems serving > 3,300 people must: complete Risk + Resilience Assessment (RRA) AND Emergency Response Plan (ERP) every 5 years. RRA addresses: malevolent acts, natural hazards, cyber, monitoring practices, financial infrastructure. ERP includes response strategies, communication plans, mitigation actions.

title: Water sector cybersecurity (post-2021)
text: Post-Oldsmar FL incident (Feb 2021). EPA + CISA joint guidance: change default credentials on all SCADA / HMI / PLC; segment OT from IT networks; multi-factor auth on remote access; sector-specific incident reporting via WaterISAC. Voluntary CWG (Cybersecurity Working Group) framework.

title: Consumer Confidence Report
text: Annual CCR required by all CWS (Community Water Systems). Delivery: by July 1 to all bill-paying customers. Content: source water, definitions, table of detected contaminants vs MCL/MCLG, violations + health effects, special-population notice (e.g. lead + young children, Cryptosporidium + immunocompromised).

title: Variance + Exemption authority
text: State (primacy agency) may grant: VARIANCE — alternative treatment when characteristics of source water make compliance technically impossible. EXEMPTION — compliance schedule extension for economic / technical reasons. Both require: public notice, monitoring, schedule. Lead + Copper, microbial rules generally NOT variance-eligible.

title: Sanitary Survey requirement
text: State primacy agency conducts on-site inspection: every 3 years for community water systems using surface water; every 5 years for groundwater. Reviews: source, treatment, distribution, monitoring, finished-water storage, operator certification, recordkeeping. Significant deficiencies must be addressed.
